heading: libro de formatione, ab eo (ut asseritur) compilato, ubi ex connotatis
card_summary: This chunk from the Fifth Tone of De Harmonia Mundi covers chapters 16 through 18 of the first Canticle, moving from mystical epistemology to cosmological arithmetic to Kabbalistic divine-name analysis. Zorzi draws on the Sefer Yetzirah (attributed to Abraham), Plato's Timaeus, Aristotle, Nicomachus, Boethius, and Augustine to argue that the universe is constructed according to number, weight, and measure (Wisdom 11:20), with the three means — arithmetic, geometric, and harmonic — corresponding to Platonic numbers, dimensions, and solids. He traces the history of musical string-theory from Mercury's four-string lyre through Terpander's seven strings to twenty-eight strings symbolizing the cosmic monochord, then adds the Tetragrammaton as the fourth completing unit to reach 32 — the thirty-two paths of wisdom in Sefer Yetzirah. Chapter 18 delivers an extended gematria of ehyeh asher ehyeh (Exodus 3:14), demonstrating that the divine Name contains twice the cubic number 27 joined by the relative asher, and that the letters he, waw, and yod of the Tetragrammaton encode the quintessence, the perfect number six (life), and the perfect number ten (divinity), all of them circular numbers that symbolize God's self-returning unity.
essay:
## Overview

Chunk 54 spans three chapters (XVI–XVIII) of the First Canticle, Fifth Tone, pages 728–750 of Campanini's Bompiani edition. The section opens with a transitional paragraph on Abraham and the *Sefer Yetzirah* as the highest vehicle of cosmological knowledge, moves through an elaborate numerical cosmology built on the triad number/measure/weight, surveys the history of musical string-theory as a microcosm of cosmic harmony, and culminates in a dense Kabbalistic analysis of the divine names *ehyeh asher ehyeh* and the Tetragrammaton. The central thesis is that the entire created order, from the nine angelic choirs through the nine heavens to the nine orders of sublunary things, is the cubic unfolding of the divine Ternary, and that God inscribed this structure into the letters and numerical values of His own supreme Name.

---

## The Sefer Yetzirah and Abrahamic Gnosis (pp. 728–730)

The chunk opens in mid-sentence, recapping the *Libro della formazione* (*Sefer Yetzirah*), said to have been compiled by Abraham himself. Zorzi explains that the book uses the letters of Hebrew names, combined with their rotations (galgal), permutations, numerical equivalents (gematria), vowel points, and cantillation accents to point toward the quiddities of things:

> «sulla base delle caratteristiche delle lettere che formano i nomi in ebraico, per mezzo delle loro rotazioni e permutazioni, delle cifre equivalenti alle lettere, nonché dei punti vocalici e degli accenti, si ha un sistema di allusioni alle quiddità delle cose e alle loro corrispondenze, una tecnica degna di meraviglia, più adatta alla contemplazione che alla spiegazione particolareggiata.»

<cite>p. 728–729</cite>

This passage situates Kabbalistic letter-mysticism as a contemplative rather than merely discursive art. It follows a rich epistemological preamble (just before this chunk begins) in which Zorzi contrasts Platonic anamnesis, Aristotelian tabula rasa, and the "death of a kiss" (*mors osculi*) taught by the most secret theologians — citing 1 John 2:20 and 2:27, John 14:26, Psalms 116:15, and 2 Corinthians 3:18. Plotinus's teaching on purification leading to union with the *primo genitore* is invoked, and Augustine's distinction between *cognitio matutina* (knowledge in God) and *cognitio vespertina* (knowledge of things in themselves) frames the contemplative epistemology that underwrites the whole chapter.

---

## Chapter XVI: Number, Weight, and Measure (pp. 730–742)

### The Three Platonic Means

Taking Wisdom 11:20 ("omnia in numero, pondere et mensura disposuisti") as his scriptural anchor, Zorzi maps this triad onto Plato's *Timaeus*:

- **Number** → arithmetic mean → specific forms of things (also in Aristotle)
- **Measure/dimension** → geometric mean → powers attributed to each thing (Matthew 25:15: "unicuique dedit secundum propriam mensuram")
- **Weight/solid** → harmonic mean → inclinations of things toward good or evil; Augustine's *pondus meum amor meus* (Confessions XIII.9)

> «Ancora, per mezzo dei numeri si indica la media aritmetica; tramite le dimensioni, o misure, la media geometrica; attraverso i pesi infine, la media armonica, che comprende con perfetto equilibrio o analogia la velocità e la lentezza dei movimenti nonché l'altezza o la gravità delle voci e le inclinazioni delle cose, ora più pesanti, ora più lievi.»

<cite>p. 733</cite>

### The Cube of the Universe

Zorzi then develops the image of the cosmic cube: just as a geometric cube drawn from three numbers (linear, planar, solid) and bounded by four surfaces generates near-infinite lines from center to circumference, so the universe, proceeding from the Ternary to 27 (3³), contains innumerable forms. The seven planets and their presiding intelligences (*ofanim*), together with the twelve signs of the Zodiac (since 3×4 = 4×3 = 12), govern the distribution of these forms. The number 7 arises from 4 surfaces + 3 numbers, and the threefold sevenary — celestial, angelic, divine — orchestrates the whole.

### History of Musical Strings as Cosmological Allegory

Zorzi narrates the expansion of the Greek lyre from four strings to twenty-eight as a mirror of cosmic structure, drawing on **Nicomachus of Gerasa** (*Enchiridion harmonices*):

- 4 strings (Mercury's invention): Hypate = earth, Parhypate = water, Nete = fire, Paranete = air
- 5th string: Coroebus of Lydia
- 6th: Hyagnis the Phrygian
- 7th: Terpander of Lesbos (equaling the seven planets)
- 8th: Lycaon; 9th: Prophrastus Periotes (nine spheres / first Ennead); 10th: Hestiaeus of Colophon; 11th: Timotheus of Miletus
- Up to 15th (bisdiapason) via semitones
- Up to 28th by mixing enharmonic, chromatic, and diatonic genera

The twenty-eight strings encode the three Enneads (3 × 9 = 27) plus one — the soul, "l'unica vita che riempie tutte le cose," citing Ezekiel 1:20 ("lo spirito di vita era nelle ruote"). The world instrument thus resonates with a single breath: one soul binding angels, heavenly bodies, and corruptible creatures. The full arithmetic is extended: not only 3→27 but 6→162 and 384→10068 (previously established expansions).

---

## Chapter XVII: The Four Causes and the Twenty-Two Hebrew Letters (pp. 742–752)

### Aristotle's Deficiency

Zorzi credits Aristotle with identifying the four causes (material, formal, efficient, final) but finds him inadequate on the efficient cause in particular: he could say only that "God and nature operate this way," without explaining the instruments or timing of divine action. Because Aristotle could not grasp the world's beginning, he was forced to postulate its eternity.

### Abraham's Superior Doctrine: Three Elements, Seven Doubles, Twelve Simples

Those instructed by a better teacher — above all **Abraham** — understand more deeply. The *Sefer Yetzirah* posits:

- **Three mother letters** (alef, mem, shin — שמא): corresponding to fire, water, and earth (air is not a material element but a *glutinum*, a binding spirit)
- **Seven double letters** (bet, gimel, dalet, kaf, peh, resh, tav — תרפכדגב): corresponding to the seven planets governed by the *Ophanim* (angelic wheels), with dual pronunciation (dagesh/rafe) reflecting the planets' being in their own or a foreign house
- **Twelve simple letters**: corresponding to the twelve zodiacal signs

> «In tal modo distribuirono tutte le lettere del loro alfabeto, che sono ventidue. Mescolandole e permutandole, formano parole incomprensibili, con le quali designano gli innumerevoli corpi che si realizzano mediante le figure piane e solide che abbiamo ricordato in precedenza e le loro combinazioni.»

<cite>p. 747</cite>

### Adam's Naming, Vowel Points, and Cantillation

Each composite thing is named by three letters drawn from the three dominant causes in its composition (element, planet, zodiacal sign). This explains how Adam knew the proper name for each creature (Genesis 2:19–20). Vowel points (*puncta*) animate the letters as life animates bodies; cantillation accents (*accentus*) represent the ten divine sources (*numi* / *sefirot*) and their channels. Adding the ten to the twenty-two gives thirty-two — the thirty-two paths of wisdom of Sefer Yetzirah: "In trentadue sentieri di sapienza Dio benedetto creò il suo mondo."

### The Tetragrammaton as Completing Fourth

The difference between 32 and 28 (the completed monochord) is 4 — the Tetragrammaton (YHWH), called *quadriliterum* in Latin. Its presence perfects, completes, and beautifies. Citing Jeremiah 23:24 ("Ego coelum et terram impleo"), Zorzi notes that whenever Moses describes creation as complete, both Elohim and the Tetragrammaton appear together; Elohim alone suffices for individual creative acts. At the creation of man — the "perfect bond of the whole world" — YHWH Elohim acts as plasmator (Genesis 2:7). The Tetragrammaton resides in Tiphereth (*תפארת*), the sixth Sefirah:

> «Poiché è la sorgente e l'origine di ogni armonia e di qualsiasi bellezza, ha nell'Archetipo la propria sede particolare; secondo la teologia degli ebrei, in תפארת, tipheret, che significa la bellezza, la gloria, la maestà, l'assoluta perfezione e l'armonia assolutamente compiuta, che a noi non è dato esprimere in alcun modo.»

<cite>p. 752</cite>

---

## Chapter XVIII: The Three Enneads Contained in *Ehyeh Asher Ehyeh* (pp. 752–762)

### The Trinity Generating Three Enneads

Zorzi ascends to the supreme principle. From *Keter* (the "Corona suprema," identified with the Father) emanates the Son (Wisdom/Hokhmah), and from both proceeds the Spirit — completing the "first and true Ternary." The Trinity then squares itself to produce nine angelic choirs, squares again for nine heavens, and squares once more for nine sublunary orders: 3×9 = 27, the perfect cube, beyond which the Ternary cannot extend.

### Gematria of *Ehyeh Asher Ehyeh*

The divine Name revealed to Moses at the Burning Bush (Exodus 3:14) — אהיה אשר אהיה (*ehyeh asher ehyeh*) — encodes the double cube 27:

- Each אהיה = alef (1) + he (5) + yod (10) + he (5) = **21**
- אשר = alef (1) + shin (300 reduced) + resh (200 reduced)... but Zorzi uses the *small number* method: alef = 1, shin = 3 (since ש has three branches), resh = 2 → total **6**
- First word (21) + middle word (6) = **27**; middle word (6) + third word (21) = **27**

> «Ecco, dunque, in che modo il 27 compare due volte in quel Nome; esso denota sia il numero divino che, non allontanandosi mai dall'unità, è l'idea e la sorgente di tutti i numeri, sia il numero delle creature, che si distingue e si lascia contare nei vari generi delle cose.»

<cite>p. 757</cite>

The middle word *asher* (relative pronoun "who/which") joins the two 27s: just as God's operations toward creation are expressed through relational names (Creator, Rector, Formator), so the grammar of the divine Name encodes the divine-creaturely relation.

### The Letters of the Tetragrammaton as Circular Numbers

The letters he, waw, yod of YHWH correspond to the three *circular numbers* (numbers whose powers always end in themselves): 5 (he), 6 (waw), 10 (yod). These signify:
- **He (5)**: the quintessence embedded in all four-element composites
- **Waw (6)**: the perfect number; the life by which all things live
- **Yod (10)**: the perfect number; divinity itself filling all things

In *ehyeh asher ehyeh*, waw is replaced by alef — signifying that the Named One never departs from circular unity. Alef (=1, also = 1000) is itself the principle and end of all numbers: units → tens → hundreds → thousands, all circular, all returning to alef.

### Boethius and Universal Harmony

Zorzi closes by invoking Boethius's definition of harmony (*De musica*): "harmonia et consonantia est parium, dispariumque vocum, in unum redacta concordia." Translated to the cosmic scale, harmony is the concordance of all genera, species, and individuals — equal and unequal — reduced to the one. God, supremely One yet diffused through all creatures, never departs from his proper unity, just as the number one, distributed through all numbers, never ceases to be one:

> «l'intero strumento armonioso del mondo, procedendo dall'uno, reso sommamente consonante dall'unica vita e dall'unica condizione che gli è propria, tenda e ritorni all'uno.»

<cite>p. 762</cite>

---

## Technical Terms Introduced

- *Mors osculi* (death of a kiss) — mystical union with God
- *Cognitio matutina / vespertina* — Augustine's distinction between archetypal and creaturely knowledge
- *Glutinum* — air as binding spirit among the three material principles
- *Ophanim* (אופנים) — the angelic wheels of Ezekiel, governing the seven planets
- *Tipheret* (תפארת) — the sixth Sefirah; beauty, harmony, perfection
- *Ehyeh asher ehyeh* (אהיה אשר אהיה) — "I am who I am," Exodus 3:14
- *Quadriliterum* / Tetragrammaton — the four-letter Name YHWH
- *Numeri circulares* — numbers (5, 6, 10) whose powers terminate in themselves

## Connections to Zorzi's Larger Project

This chunk is the arithmetical and onomastic climax of the Fifth Tone. The cosmic cube (27) established through numerological analysis of *ehyeh asher ehyeh* is the quantitative backbone of the entire *De Harmonia Mundi*: it links the Trinitarian theology of Canticle I to the Sefirotic map of Canticle II and the Christological synthesis of Canticle III. The identification of YHWH with Tiphereth — the central Sefirah, seat of beauty and harmony — aligns the Christian Son/Word with the Kabbalistic Mediator, anticipating Zorzi's Christ-as-cosmic-harmony argument. The musical string history, from Mercury's tetrachord to the twenty-eight-string world-monochord, performs the same function visually and acoustically that the gematria performs arithmetically: both demonstrate that the universe is a single instrument tuned by the One who both generates and returns all things to unity.
